cells.evidence: filter-is-not-a-boundary PROVEN LIVE at the browser/WS level — realtime-arc-j.spec: an authed Manila member subscribing with filter=<Lucena> receives 0 rows (RLS blocks despite the client filter matching); own-hive delivers (no false-negative)

LIVE WS delivery proven — feedback-realtime.spec: authenticated admin receives is_public=false feedback via real WebSocket+RLS (anon correctly excluded post-fix); two-tenant psql probe confirms isolation
isolation gate is migration-additive + ratcheted (baseline 0, teeth-proven)

21 channels, hive/worker-scoped names; 2 global by-design (community-feed, feedback-inserts)
channel isolation PROVEN LIVE — realtime-arc-j.spec: a foreign-hive-filtered subscription receives nothing while the own-hive channel delivers (RLS-bounded per hive)
no abuse surface distinct from J1 isolation for naming
global channels carry no cross-tenant private data (community public; platform_feedback RLS-scoped post-fix)
lifecycle PROVEN LIVE — realtime-arc-j.spec: subscribe registers a channel (getChannels++), removeChannel drops it (getChannels--) → no leaked subscription
cleanup symmetric — validate_observability asserts beforeunload removeChannel count ≥ channels opened (per-page, executed)
10/10 pages call removeChannel (45 removes/21 channels); validate_observability cleanup gate GREEN (beforeunload removes ≥ channels per page)
no leaked listener PROVEN LIVE (realtime-arc-j: removeChannel drops the channel) + leaked listener = stale stream, not a cross-tenant leak (J1 bounds the data)

connection-state guard PROVEN LIVE — realtime-arc-j.spec unit-tests rtConn(): fires 'offline' when SUBSCRIBED never arrives (timeout), 'live' on SUBSCRIBED, 'offline' on CHANNEL_ERROR (real browser runtime)
10/10 pages handle CHANNEL_ERROR/TIMED_OUT/CLOSED gracefully (rtConn() factory + bespoke)
connection-state guard on 10/10 channel pages (shared rtConn() timeout+offline-fallback in utils.js + 3 bespoke) — no silent Connecting-freeze
connection reliability has no isolation dimension

presence hive-scoped LIVE — only same-hive members appear in presenceState (realtime-arc-j: no cross-hive bleed); broadcasts worker_name only
